title: thalictrum minus subsp. calcareum
intro: Thalictrum minus subsp. calcareum, also known as meadow rue, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Ranunculaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be attributed to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of meadow rue include its ability to reduce swelling and pain, as well as its potential to act as an antimicrobial agent, making it useful in treating various skin conditions and wounds. Meadow rue can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used externally or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
